text.title: django-service-layer Worked Example
text.summary: End-to-end example: order creation across Order + OrderItem with notification fired only on commit.

example.code:
# apps/orders/services.py from decimal import Decimal from django.db import transaction from apps.orders.models import Order, OrderItem from apps.orders.tasks import notify_user_order_created from core.exceptions import ValidationError
def order_create(*, user, items: list[dict], shipping_method_id: int) -> Order: if not items: raise ValidationError("order must contain at least one item") total = sum(Decimal(str(i["price"])) * i["quantity"] for i in items)
with transaction.atomic(): order = Order(user=user, shipping_method_id=shipping_method_id, total=total) order.full_clean() order.save() OrderItem.objects.bulk_create([ OrderItem(order=order, product_id=i["product_id"], quantity=i["quantity"], price=i["price"]) for i in items ])
transaction.on_commit( lambda: notify_user_order_created.delay(order_id=order.id) )
return order

# apps/orders/tests/test_services.py import pytest from django.db import transaction from apps.orders.services import order_create from apps.orders.models import Order, OrderItem
@pytest.mark.django_db(transaction=True) def test_order_create_rolls_back_on_full_clean_failure(user_factory, monkeypatch): user = user_factory() bad_items = [{"product_id": 1, "quantity": 1, "price": -10}] # negative price
with pytest.raises(Exception): order_create(user=user, items=bad_items, shipping_method_id=1)
# No Order or OrderItem rows committed assert Order.objects.count() == 0 assert OrderItem.objects.count() == 0
example.note: The negative price violates `OrderItem.full_clean()`, the atomic block rolls back, and `notify_user_order_created.delay()` never fires because `on_commit` skips on rollback.
